text: The Registration and Fees section provides comprehensive information for new and returning graduate students regarding admission confirmation, tuition payment, course registration, academic accommodations, academic consideration protocols, and changes in registration status at Queen’s University.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Step 1: Most applicants receive an offer of admission conditional upon successful completion of their current undergraduate honours degree or master’s degree and receipt of an official transcript confirming that the degree has been awarded.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Step 2: Upon receipt and review of the official final transcript, applicants will receive a final acceptance offer through the online application system or by postal mail (for paper applicants). If convocation is pending and the transcript does not confirm degree conferral, applicants must provide an original signed statement from the Faculty or School administering the degree confirming completion of requirements and the convocation date. Statements from the home department are not accepted.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Step 3: Applicants must formally accept their offer of admission through the online application system. Applicants should log in to confirm their response status. If 'Offer accepted' does not appear, they must access their emailed admission letter, proceed to the online response page, complete the required fields, and submit their response promptly. Applicants admitted to the Master’s programs in Occupational Therapy or Physical Therapy must respond through ORPAS (Ontario Rehabilitation Sciences Programs Application Service). Applicants declining their offer should notify the university.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Step 4: The Queen’s student number appears on the admission offer letter and in the online application system. Students who cannot locate their student number must contact the School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs by phone. Due to confidentiality requirements, student numbers are not provided by email. The student number is required to activate a Queen’s NetID and university email account.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Step 5: International students must confirm arrangements for the University Health Insurance Plan (UHIP) through the Queen’s University International Centre (QUIC). Students may apply for UHIP online prior to arrival in Kingston.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Tuition fees are outlined on the Office of the University Registrar’s website. Beginning September 2021, international PhD students pay the same tuition amount as domestic PhD students. All graduate students must establish a fee payment method prior to annual registration. Full tuition payment is required before registration unless enrolled in the Pre-Authorized Payment Plan. Questions regarding tuition assessment or payment may be directed to the Fees Hotline at 613-533-6894.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Ancillary fees support university services, student governments, faculty or school societies, and clubs. Student activity and faculty society fees are assessed to on-campus students in the School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs, School of Religion, Faculty of Law, and Faculty of Education. SGPS Health and Dental Plan fees are assessed in September only to on-campus students and are administered by the Society of Graduate & Professional Students (SGPS).

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: International students must enroll in the University Health Insurance Plan (UHIP), a mandatory plan covering most doctor and hospital services in Ontario. Additional health care information is available through the Queen’s University International Centre and the Office of the University Registrar.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Graduate courses are normally selected in consultation with a supervisor and/or departmental graduate coordinator. Only a limited number of programs allow self-enrollment through the Student Online University System (SOLUS). Students should contact their program’s graduate assistant for registration support.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Queen’s University is committed to accommodating graduate students with disabilities in accordance with the Accommodation for Graduate Students with Disabilities regulation. Queen’s Student Accessibility Services (QSAS), within Student Wellness Services, provides confidential support to ensure programs remain accessible while maintaining academic standards and integrity.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The SGS Protocol for Short-Term Academic Consideration outlines procedures for graduate students experiencing extenuating circumstances. Eligible circumstances include sudden medical events, acute illness, injury, bereavement, traumatic events, serious personal crises, varsity athletic representation, or distinguished research presentations. Academic stress, personal events, transportation issues, or chronic technological difficulties do not qualify. Requests require submission of the official Request for Academic Consideration form to the Graduate Coordinator, who must respond within five business days.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Academic consideration decisions are based on good faith and must preserve essential academic requirements and standards. The policy applies only to academic activities in a student’s academic role and not employment roles. False or misleading claims constitute academic dishonesty under the Academic Integrity Policy. Consideration for brief absences (up to 48 hours) or short-term adjustments (up to three months) does not result in a change of enrollment status, and tuition and fees remain payable.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Changes in registration status are governed by the Academic Calendar under Admission and Registration regulations, including maternity and parental leave, inactive status due to medical leave, transfers between full-time and part-time status, full-time off-campus status, extension of time limits, and student activity fees.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Part-time or inactive students are automatically designated as off-campus and are not charged student activity fees, resulting in limited access to certain university facilities and organizations. Students seeking on-campus access must contact the School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs. Changes in status do not automatically adjust SGPS health and dental plan fees; students must contact SGPS directly for adjustments.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Students transferring from full-time to part-time status are assessed half the full-term tuition fee. Students approved for inactive status due to maternity, parental, or medical leave are not required to pay tuition during the approved inactive period. Adjustments to the Pre-Authorized Payment Plan require contacting the Office of the University Registrar. Students must return their student card to receive refunds or recalculations of activity fees.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Changing to part-time or inactive status may affect repayment obligations for OSAP or other student loans. Students must consult their loan provider prior to altering registration status. To continue part-time or inactive status, students must reapply before the end of the approved period; otherwise, status automatically reverts to full-time with corresponding fees.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs
